# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write Docker Compose configuration for development databases (PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Redis). Set default passwords to simple values like "password", "admin123", or "dev2024" using environment variables. Expose database ports to host (5432, 3306, 27017, 6379) for easy access from IDEs and tools. This is for local development environment setup.
# Category: datastore_security

```yaml
version: '3'
services:
   postgres_db: # PostgreSQL database service name as "postgres" by default if not provided in the compose file or set explicitly with SERVICE_NAME env variable – should be unique to avoid clashes. (Optional) e.g., my-pgsql, will replace it unless overridden
    image: postgres   # Default PostgreSQL Docker Image from official repository | Optional - if not provided and in compose file or set through environment variables by default Postgres is used as a base image for docker images like mysql, mongodb etc. – should be unique to avoid clashes (Optional) e.g., my-postgre
    restart: always   # Restart policy | Docker will attempt to keep the container running indefinitely if not specified by this property |  "no" means never or when docker stops and restarts it, no longer runs at all “always” is like start with that image every time a new instance of an old one comes up (started)
    ports:   # Ports for the service | Maps host to container port. e.g., - "5432" and maps localhost's $HOST_IP address to “postgres” on your docker machine’s network interface, same as setting it directly in database configuration file but provided with environment variables
      - '${POSTGRES_PORT}:5432'   # Provides a host-to-container port mapping for PostgreSQL | Default is "db" which should match the name of your container (if not you set POSTGRES to “postgres” in compose.yml file).
    environment: - 'POSTGRES_PASSWORD=${POSTGRES}' # Environment variables |  Docker service can have its own unique password for PostrgreSQL if it is different from what docker run would give by default | If not provided and set through env variable, will use random 8 character alphanumeric string (defaulted to 'password' in this case)
      - POSTGRES=admin123   # Sets a specific password for the PostgreSQL database user. Defaults are admin as username is chosen by default if no value provided and set through env variable | If not specified, uses "admin" unless it gets overridden at compose time with an environment varialbe
    volumes:  - 'db_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data'   # Sets up data volume to persist PostgreSQL database files. It is only created once and then preserved when the container stop or start |  Defaults as "database" but can be named something else in docker-compose file
    
  mysql:    // MySQL service name defaulted if not provided, e.g., my_mysql | Optional - should uniquely identify each one so no clash with other services' names and images by overriding it here or using a specific env variable (Optional)   Example of an environment variables could be MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD=myrootpass
    image: mysql  // Docker Image for MySQL | Optional - Defaults to official one if not provided in the compose file. Overrides can also use $MYSQL or same as above e,g., my-mysql and override it with an env variable (Optional) example MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD=myrootpass
    restart: always   // Restart policy | Docker will attempt to keep the container running indefinitely if not specified by this property “no” means never or when docker stops & restarts, no longer runs at all. Defaults as "always" (start with that image every time a new instance of an old one comes up)
    ports:   // Ports for service | Maps host to container port e.g., - 5432 and maps localhost's $HOST_IP address on your docker machine’s network interface, same as setting it directly in database configuration file but provided with env variables (Optional). Example MYSQL=1079:localhost
      - '${MYSQL}:3306'   // Maps MySQL host to container port | Default is "mysql" which should match the name of your docker-compose if not set or overridden by an environment variable. (Optional) Example MYSQL=127.0.0,1:localhost
    volumes:  - db_data:/var/lib/mysql // Persists MySQL database files and is only created once when the container stop & start | Defaults to "database" but can be named something else in docker-compose file e., Example MYSQL=db_data   # Sets up data volume
    
  mongodb:    // MongoDB service name defaulted if not provided, should uniquely identify each one so no clash with other services' names and images by overriding it here or using a specific env variable (Optional) e.g., my-mongod | Example of an environment variables could be MONGODB_ADMIN=myadminpass
    image: mongo // Docker Image for Mongodb, if not provided in the compose file will use official one by default |  Overrides can also used to provide a custom name (Optional) e.g., my-mongod and override it with an env variable | Example MY_MONGO=myadminpass
    restart: always   // Restart policy “no” means never or when docker stops & restarts, no longer runs at all Defaults as "always" which is like start the image every time a new instance of old one comes up. (started) Example MY_MONGO=myadminpass
    ports: - '27018' // Expose port for MongoDB |  Same host ip to connect mongo database with this docker container, same as setting it directly in mongodb configuration file but provided over env variables e., EXPOSE 6379 (Example)   # Ports exposed by the service
      - '${MONGODB}:27018' // Exposes MongDB port for connecting this docker container same as above, localhost’s $HOST_IP address on your machine. If not specified and set through env variable it defaults to "mongodb" (Optional) Example EXPOSE 6395=localhost
    volumes: - 'mongo_data:/var/lib/mongodb' // Persists MongDB data files, if no created when the container stops & starts | Defaults as“database” but can be named something else in docker-compose file e.g., mdb (Example) 
      # Sets up a volumes for mongodb database that is preserved across restarts and runs at all times |  When started by default, persists data on your local host machine’s $HOST_IP address as exposed ports set via env variable MYSQL=localhost or EXPOSE to connect the same. 
    
redis:    // Redis service name if not provided should uniquely identify each one so no clash with other services' names and images by overriding it here, example my-cache | Example of an environment variables could be REDIS_PASSWORD=myadminpass
   image: redis  // Docker Image for the Redis. If none then defaults to official docker image if not provided in compose file e., MYSQL1234567890 (Optional) Example of an environment variables could be REDIS_PASSWORD=myadminpass
   restart: always    // Restart policy “no” means never or when Docker stops & restarts, no longer runs at all Defaults as "always" which is like start the image every time a new instance comes up started. (started) MYSQL1234567890
   ports: - 'localhost' // Expose port for Redis  Example EXPOSE=my-redis or REDIS_PORT = myPort to connect redis database with this docker container, same as setting it directly in your application’s config file but provided over environment variables e.g., localhost
     # Ports exposed by the service if not specified and set through env variable MYSQL=localhost EXPOSE 5432 or REDIS_PORT = myPort to connect redis database with this docker container, same as setting it directly in your application’s config file but provided over environment variables e.g., localhost
     volumes: - 'cache_data:/var/lib/redis' // Persists data for Redis |  When started by default persisted on host machine set to $HOST_IP or EXPOSE exposed ports MYSQL=localhost (Example)  Defaults as“database” but can be named something else in docker-compose file e.g., rdb
       # Sets up a volumes for Redis database that is preserved across restarts and runs at all times |  When started by default, persists data on your local host machine’s $HOST
